name: Bagwell, Carl I.
motto: "On his piercing eye Sat observation; on each glance of thought Decision follow'd".
description: We refuse to believe Professor Bell's Political Science, or the Phi Sigma Literary Society could continue to operate without this "oracle of wisdom." Not a prince in banquet halls, but a capable, hardworking fellow.

name: Bell, H. H.
motto: "Whate'er he did was done with so much ease, In him alone 'twas natural to please."
description: An ex-ladies' man, school teacher, and warrior. A serious hardworking student, who places his classes first in his attention, yet who has as much fun as the next fellow.

name: Butler, Henry Anderson
motto: "Unmoved, calm and serene he walks, He has the gait of a tractor engine."
description: Not endowed with the beauty of Apollo, but a man of keen intellect, pleasing personality, and aggressive force. His fondest dream is that of a cozy bungalow on the banks of the Susquehanna with (deleted).

name: Butler, Stewart Harrison
motto: "Labor conquereth all."
description: A steady, industrious student. A man who would weep if his grades fell below A, which he never did. Cupid's darts will fall harmlessly about him.

name: Carnathan, James Wilson
motto: "A man who seems of cheerful yesterdays And confident to-morrows."
description: A local boy whom we are glad to number among our friends in town and to whom we are indebted for many kindnesses.

name: Carney, Andre Blythe
motto: "They are never alone that are accompanied with noble thoughts."; "He hath o'er his books consumed the midnight oil."
clubs: Vice President A. S. B. O. '20
description: Small in stature, but big in heart, Alpha is one of the most popular fellows on the campus. Whether climbing flag-poles, bewildering opposing batteries, or making love, he excels in all. A man with no enemies, but many friends.; "Alpha" is a social star, an athlete and a student. By his genial, lovable traits he has made all who know him love him—especially the Co-eds.

name: Cheek, Eliza Sommerville
motto: "Happy am I, from care I am free, Why aren't they all contented like me."
description: One whom the graces have smiled upon. Lovable by nature and studious by inclination—she is one entitled to an opinion of her own. Her outstanding trait is her love for Sophomore Physics.

name: Cochran, Edward Louis
motto: "One who loves the odor of powder and perfume."
clubs: Chief Hobo A. A. A. H. '19
description: His efforts at scribbling, his love (?) of hard work and uncensored vocal propensities distinguish him as one set apart from the rest of mankind.

name: Cowart, Edmund M.
motto: "All his faults are such that one loves him the better for them."; "A combination, and a form, indeed, Where every god did seem to set his seal, To give the world assurance of a man."
description: A gentle-tongued, modest, handsome fellow whose looks have made many a fair maiden sigh. Always near the top whether on the Athletic field, or in the classroom.; A diligent student, an aggressive football player, and a popular man. His only weakness is his handsome face, and that bothers him not at all.

name: Creekmore, Rufus Horton
motto: "Ability wins us the esteem of true men; luck that of the people."
clubs: Chairman Red and Blue Dance Committee
description: Being from Water Valley and professing an acquaintance with E. M. Cowart were his greatest drawbacks. "Benny" is a ladies' man, long, graceful, and admirable to even himself.

name: Culpepper, Sebron C.
motto: "Merit was ever modest known."; "For just experience tells, in every soil, That those that think must govern those that toil."
description: The lad from South Miss. with the tailor-made shape. Cully was never known to eat more than twenty-seven skids and utterly abhors anything stronger than water.; The favorite of the professors and the ladies. A man to pattern after in dress, and to envy in looks. A popular and industrious student.

name: Davis, J. Preston
motto: "Doubt that the Sun doth move; But never doubt I love."; "Thanks to Him who first invented work."
description: Day before yesterday he had his hair parted in the center. He always gets the front row at all musical comedies and never fails to carry opera glasses.; A man of few words and wistful yearnings. A steady supporter of the Standard Oil Company, as his grades testify.

name: Dean, Sara Ruth
motto: "All that I sought, I have, or ever seeking shall acquire."; "Well read in poetry and other books—good ones I warrant ye."
clubs: President of Alpha Theta Literary Society '20
description: Her amusements and recreations are uniformly innocuous, dissecting, poetry, etc. Space, modesty and the lady forbid us to say more.; One of our future M. D.'s. A girl with a pleasing personality, and keen intellect. Her only failing is writing love sonnets.

name: Doxey, Hindman
motto: "If there's delight in love, 'tis when I see That heart, which others bleed for, bleeds for me."
description: A man with Holly Springs characteristics. Tall, lean, with dreamy eyes—his is a figure to command attention. Born to command, but just as willing to follow.

name: Dunlap, Mary Mcneill
motto: "Can wisely tell what hour o' the day, The clock does strike by algebra."
clubs: Student Assistant in Bacteriology and Pathology 1918-19, 1919-20
description: One who achieves what she desires. An authority on Mathematics and Bacteriology. A lady with rare accomplishments and enviable record.

name: England, Bryan
motto: "I am not in the roll of common men."
description: He stands out as a man to be followed—first in all he undertakes. A Rhodes Scholar from Ole Miss, he is at present in Oxford, England. A great future awaits his entrance.

name: George, A. P.
motto: "Why all this toil and trouble?"
description: Quiet, unassuming, a man of few words and strong feeling. A splendid athlete, a good student, and one with all the ear-marks of success.

name: Giles, Frances
motto: "I value science—none can prize it more; It gives ten thousand motives to adore."
description: Small in stature, sweet in disposition, and brilliant in mind she is one whom many admire and none dislike. An omnivorous reader of novels and home-brewed poetry.

name: Harvey, F. T.
motto: "Who shall decide, when doctors disagree, And soundest casuists doubt, like you and me?"
clubs: Grub Mooter, A. A. A. H. 18-19
Chief Hobo, A. A. A. H. 19-20
Secretary and Treasurer, Junior Medical Class 19-20
description: Big in body and soul, jovial in disposition and keen in intellect. One of the best-liked fellows on the campus, with a weakness for Freshman Math.

name: Hawks, S. H.
motto: "There is no courage, but in innocence, no constancy but in an honest cause."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Council and Cabinet
description: During his residence at Ole Miss "Cicero" has firmly established himself as a man of independent will and true merit. Modest, unassuming, but always for the right, he has a firm place in the affections of his friends.

name: Hull, William Wilson
motto: "Indulge, and to thy genius freely give, For not to live at ease is not to live."
description: Diminutive in stature, but massive in intellect. A man of voluminous correspondence, and large affairs. Apt in his studies, and direct in his dealings—his life journey should be toward the mountain top.

name: Ivy, William Purvis
motto: "I may justly say, with the Conqueror of Rome, 'I came, I saw, I conquered.'"
description: For four years he hasn't cut a single class. He has two hobbies, Physics and Mathematics, and in these he has been a "community coach" and has served well in this capacity. He has made good grades and a record to be proud of.

name: Johnson, James Arthur
motto: "In their own merits, modest men are dumb."; "Joy, temperance and repose, Slam the door on the doctor's nose."
description: He's a lamb in a crowd, girls, but oh! my! just get him alone, "you'd be surprised." Besides being a good student he is well liked among his fellow students.; A man who talks little, but does a great deal. Modest in demeanor and calm in his self assurance. A promising future lies before this Doctor.

name: Jones, Ira Fulton
motto: "He danced without theatrical pretense, Not a ballet-dancer in the van, But like a gentleman."; "Is this that haughty gallant, gay Lothario?"
description: Not a shark in Freshman Math or Sophomore Physics, but a naughty cut-up in writing love letters. One of the best-natured, genial, well-liked fellows on the Campus.; A lover of beauty wherever he finds it. A capable, dependable, and hard working lad—and a merry one.

name: Kirk, Robert Dixon, Jr.
motto: "Lest men suspect your tale untrue, Keep probability in view."; "He talked on and on and said nothing."
clubs: Chief Yegg A. A. A. H. Club
description: A tall, alert young fellow with a piercing eye. One who can make A's, play football, and take in all the shows at the same time.; A consecrated and devoted student of Medicine, a jolly good fellow, and a lean one. The fellows like him, the girls adore him, and he thinks well of himself. Nuff sed.

name: Little, Ashford Hunter
motto: "That I might live alone once with my gold, O, 'tis a sweet companion kind and true."; "And when a lady's in the case, you know all other things give place."
description: A most dependable, and wide-awake young fellow. A social lion, and a bear in his classroom work—he is a man to be reckoned with.; A lad whose favorite pastime is reading Anatomy. A believer in "American Arts"--he plays an artful hand himself-in everything.

name: McCain, Addie Elizabeth
motto: "We knew her by her beauty and wit, Her affability and bashful modesty, Her wondrous qualities and mild behavior."
description: One to whom nature has been lavish in her endowments. A girl universally esteemed and admired. One who possesses the rare virtue of talking only when she has something to say.

name: Murphree, Edward Lile
motto: "From the crown of his head to the sole of his foot he is all mirth."
description: Diminutive in form, but a Hercules with the ladies. An ardent exponent of the Terpsichorean art, and a fiend in education. Modest, quiet, and well liked by his fellows.

name: White, Lydia
motto: "Thy modesty is a candle to thy merit."
description: A girl of serene demeanor, attractive personality, and persuasive ways. As unaffected as the air—petty trials and vexations vex her not at all.

name: Newsom, Nathan William
motto: "He is, as the great poets, an ever fresh fountain of inspiration and stimulation to those who truly think."
description: "Bill" was the lad who absorbed all of the Latin and Greek that floated around the campus and then clamored for more. The ancient languages were not his only lines of endeavor as he is prominently identified with many a Freshman Nightmare. A popular lad.

name: Ray, Edward Hunt
motto: "A faultless body and a blameless mind."; "A lucky chance that often decides the fate of mighty monarchs."
description: A former Auburn lad who quickly made a record for himself on the athletic field and in the classroom. As aggressive as his red hair would imply, he stands first in the affections of (well, it's a girl, all right) and on the football field.; A merry noise-maker when there is nothing to do, but when work is in sight he does it. A giant in physique, but a dwarf in his resistance to the charms of his fair acquaintances.

name: Ray, Leighton W.
motto: "We grant, although he had much wit, He was very shy of using it."
description: This gentleman from Kosciusko admits that he was a bold rover of the sea in the late war and that he is proud of it. One of the most brilliant students in his class. He leaves behind him a remarkable record.

name: Tomlinson, James Robert
motto: "If love is a crime, then I am the meanest mortal that lives."
description: A son of Helena and worthy of the name. A conscientious student and one who can enjoy the pleasures of life without having them interfere with his scholastic work.

name: Roberts, Curtis McWhorter
motto: "A man I am, crossed with adversity."
description: It is a high compliment to any man to say of him, "He is dependable." This compliment we give unreservedly to this man who has met and overcome the tasks confronting him here and who will do the same with the problems of life.

name: Stovall, Robert Clark
motto: "The fashion wears out more apparel than the man."
description: A true Chesterfieldian. Popular among the boys for his genial nature, and adored by the girls for his handsome face. A heart smasher, but not a trifler.

name: Summers, Chester Lamar
motto: "Along the cool sequestered vale of life, He kept the noiseless tenor of his way."
clubs: President of Phi Sigma first term '19
description: "Shorty" Sumners came to "Ole Miss" from the State Normal and quickly demonstrated by his splendid work here that his preparation had been of the highest caliber. In addition to being an excellent student he was a good athlete and a masterful speaker.

name: Tankersley, Marjorie
motto: "There's nothing ill can dwell in such a temple, If the ill spirit have so fair a house, Good things will strive to dwell with 't."
description: A girl whose attractive personality has endeared her to all. One who is never happy save when making "A's" in her class-room work and she is always happy.

name: Threlkeld, James Sager
motto: "Blushes! the heart's meteor tilting in the face."
description: He believes that actions speak louder than words and has made it his task to achieve rather than to talk. His record as a student is a good one but as a lover is better.

name: Turner, Ed B.
motto: "Great works are performed not by strength but by perseverance."
description: Here we have another quiet, unassuming chap who has more than made good in his works. His only weakness is that of having no weakness at all.

name: Wendel, Mary Paine
motto: "Time for work, yet take much holiday for art's and friendship's sake."
clubs: President of the "I Should Gloom" Club
description: A girl whose cheerfulness is contagious, and one who never walks across the campus alone. She is to be a landscape (?) artist.

name: Wilson, Katie Mae
motto: "Science when well digested is nothing but good sense and reason."
description: An exceptionally good student, an exceptionally cheerful young person, an exceptionally attractive girl, and oh—an exceptional being.

name: Walker, Annie See
motto: "They prize books most who are themselves wise."
description: A demure little maiden of attractive personality and sparkling wit. One who considers she has not made a grade unless 'tis an "A plus."

name: Rowsey, James Melvin
motto: "Character is more lasting than a monument of marble."
description: Although living in town, and being deprived of the privileges of dormitory life—he is a regular fellow, and a prince of a man. We predict for him the victory in whatever he undertakes.

name: Lockard, James Nicholas
motto: "Books cannot always please, however good, Minds are not ever craving for their food."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Red and Blue Club '20
description: A believer in the Classics and in Science, a good student and a better "fellow" he numbers his friends by those who know him.

name: Hargis, Rosa Irene
motto: "Music that brings sweet sleep down from the blissful skies."
description: An industrious and well poised girl, of happy mannerisms and demeanor. Always happy and quick to see the bright side of everything.

name: Ramey, Myrtle E.
motto: "O life, I feel thee in the breeze, I feel thee bounding in my veins."
description: Her tastes in the main run to athletics and scribbling poetry. Popular among both sexes, she has learned the secret of making high grades with little effort.

name: Pigford, Russel C.
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."
description: "Framed to make woman false." He comes to us from Meridian, and during his four years' stay has won many friends and has made an excellent record. He is one of the most popular boys in school and does well that which he attempts to do.

name: Henderson, James Boyce
motto: "Marriage and hanging go by destiny; matches are made in heaven."; "It is not what you might have done, but what you do that counts."
description: A man strong in character and conscious in his belief. A hard worker, and is bound to succeed in his chosen profession.; Tall, spare and blessed with a will of his own. A man who will not be content with small matters.

name: Herbert, A. Harper
motto: "A man with whom reason reigns supreme."; "Silence is a symbol of wisdom."
description: A man whose countenance does not reveal his knowledge. One who can conceal his thoughts as well as anybody and who can express them much better than many. A good talker but a better listener.; Genial, sincere, and a born worker—he allows little knowledge to escape him. His favorite study is Biology, and he is a shark in that.

name: Taylor, Holcomb Mcelroy
motto: "Old as I am, for ladies' love unfit, The power of beauty I remember yet."
description: A man who has tasted the joys of life, and found them sweet. A man respected by both his classmates and professors. An industrious, conscientious student.

name: Norman, Leslie Reagan
motto: "That man that hath a tongue is no man, If with that tongue he cannot win a woman."; "Never too late to make a beginning, Honest effort proclaims a satisfactory ending."
description: A hard worker, a student and a married man. Could we heap higher praise upon him! Success in life awaits his bidding.; A studious man and a hard worker. One who will always get the most out of life. A pleasing word for all whom he meets and a jolly good fellow when all is said.

name: Phoenix, Films
motto: "No, I may not be so wise as I look, but I'll bet you can't find it out."

name: Conn, W. D., Jr.
clubs: 2nd Lt. U. S. Infantry, stationed at Harvard '19

name: Hathorn, John C.
description: OLE MISS

name: Herring, George B.
description: OLE MISS

name: Hougland, Horace J.
description: OLE MISS

name: Jiggitts, Louis M.
description: OLE MISS

name: Langston, Judson Davis
description: OLE MISS

name: Lester, William Drane
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Cabinet and Council
Representative in M. I. O. A.
description: OLE MISS

name: Lipscomb, Hubert Slaton
description: OLE MISS

name: Hood, C. R.
description: OLE MISS

name: Houston, James R.
description: OLE MISS

name: Thompson, McClellan
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Council and Cabinet
Circulation and Assistant Business Manager of Mississippian '20

name: William E., Potts
clubs: Student Assistant in Commerce and Business Administration '20

name: Arnold Henry, Scott
clubs: Delegate to Students' Volunteer Convention, Des Moines '20

name: Turner, Ashby Woodson
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Council A. A. A. H.

name: Ross, E. Anderson
motto: "He never says a foolish thing, nor ever does a wise one."
major: M. D., B. A. Mississippi College
description: A former Mississippi College man who has seen the light. A serious student who takes life as he finds it, and allows his troubles to come to him.

name: Burns, Edgar
motto: "'Tis not in mortals to command success, But we'll do more, we'll deserve it."
description: A lad with a long body and a "long" head. Always on the job—he never fails to stand in the forefront of his class.

name: Cooper, Robert W.
motto: "A man in all the world's new fashion planted, That hath a mint of phrases in his brain."
description: A capable man with a "piercing and discerning eye." One who idles not, but toils the whole day through.

name: Giles, Frances Ethel
motto: "If she will she will you may depend on it, And if she won't she won't so this is the end on 't."
clubs: Vice-President of Alpha Theta Literary Society '20
description: A girl whose delight is to excel her classmates. A young lady of many attainments whose only weakness is reading her classmates sonnets.

name: Gordon, James Otis
motto: "My only books were woman's looks, And folly's all they've taught me."
major: M. D., B. S. Mississippi College, 1918
description: A man who combines good sense with good nature and with a laugh that would make the welkin ring in sympathy. Very popular and esteemed among the student body.

name: Kimbrough, Robert Alexander
motto: "He had the one great quality of excellence-- stability."
description: A Mississippi College product transplanted to Ole Miss. Already a surgeon of note, he delights to practice upon whatever is nearest at hand.

name: Morris, Lawrence B.
motto: "In the lexicon of youth, which fate reserves, for a bright manhood, there is no such word as fail."
clubs: Varsity Football, Basket Ball and Track in '18 at Mississippi
description: As an athlete and a student he has won well-deserved honors. A capable and popular fellow he will secure that for which he strives.

name: Schwartz, Robert E.
motto: "I, thus neglecting worldly ends, all dedicated To closeness and the bettering of my mind."
major: B. S. University of Mississippi, 1919; M. D.
description: His belief in Ole Miss is aptly shown by the fact that he brought his younger brother with him, and his success in both literary and medical work is one that should cause him to feel justly proud.

name: Threlkeld, James S.
motto: "He conquers by diligent effort and constant perseverance."
description: A thorough student who puts his heart into his work, on the theory that the more he puts into it the more he will get out. A man worth watching.

name: James, Shelby Shipman
clubs: Representative to Student's Volunteer Convention, Des Moines, Iowa

name: Harvey, Frank Tate
clubs: Grub Moocher A. A. A. H. '19
Chief Hobo A. A. A. H. '20

name: Gerard, H. Brandon
motto: "The workman is known by his works."
clubs: Chairman, Honor Council Junior Law Class '17
description: As District Attorney of University County, he has proven to his constituents that his success as a lawyer is assured, for verily "By their fruits ye shall know them."

name: Adrian, Latham Brunson
motto: "Deeper than did ever plummet sound."
description: If "As the twig is bent, the tree's inclined," we see in the future a sober judge, whose knowledge of the law is profound and whose execution of it is amazing.

name: Robert, Jefferson Coker, Jr.
motto: "Let us consider the reason of the case for nothing is law that is not reason."
major: LL. B., B. S. Mississippi A. & M. '18
clubs: Chairman, Honor Council Junior Law Class '19
description: Quietly unassuming, yet forceful, this native of Yazoo City has made himself known as a man to be reckoned with. A man with no enemies, but many friends.

name: James, Tanner Crawley, Jr.
motto: "Let any man speak long enough and he will get believers."
major: LL. B., B. S. with distinction, '19
description: Truly a man to whom no field of knowledge is foreign, yet one whose greatest work has been for his Alma Mater, for "whom greater love hath no man."

name: Crenshaw, James W.
motto: "I confess to the sweetest of crimes, A fair maiden, woo'd and won."
description: A man whose opinion is respected by all. As sober as a judge, but withal as jolly as a cockney cabman. Watch him come out on the top in the scramble of life.

name: Denton, Clifton Lamar
motto: "Seven hours to law, to soothing slumber seven, ten to the world allot."
description: When pleading a legal case, his greatest attainment, he puts his soul into his utterances, all of which is good training for Cupid's case for which he is soon to plead.

name: Fox, John Henry Jr.
motto: "Young in limbs, in judgment old."
description: A sly one too, for in all cases both legal and otherwise, no one has ever yet been able to catch him when he was looking the other way.

name: Garner, Martin
motto: "He who should search for pearls must dive below."
description: During his one year at the University Law School he has shown himself a lawyer of high caliber and unquestioned worth. A man that can be depended upon and a man who has a bright future before him.

name: Hilzim, William Harrington
motto: "Strength of heart and might of limb, Are winners in this pastime."
description: A tall, athletic fellow with a genial smile. His popularity among his fellow students, and ability as a lawyer augurs well for his future success.

name: Hester, Clyde Lanier
motto: "Ev'n to the dullest peasant standing by He seem'd the master spirit of the land."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Council '17, Cabinet '18
description: A born orator and a wide-awake law student endowed with a keen intellect and magnetic personality. One of the most popular students in school.

name: Holmes, David Warren
motto: "But still his tongue ran on, the less of weight it bore with greater ease."
description: A jolly good fellow, and one with real worth. An "ex-all-southern" twirler, popular among his fellows, and one who knows women—his only regret is his state of bachelorhood.

name: Mayfield, Charles Russell
motto: "Owe no man anything, but to love one another; for he that loveth another hath fulfilled the law."
description: A man esteemed by all. One who puts his best in all he undertakes whether it be managing the local picture show or a Moot Court case.

name: Percy, D. Liddell
motto: "A young man married, is a man that's marr'd."
description: A true friend, a hard worker, and a good one. A man who expresses himself forcefully in a few words.

name: Montgomery, Felix Bowden
motto: "An ounce of reality is worth a pound of romance."
description: One of the strong men of the Senior Class. A man who goes to the heart of matters in all he undertakes. Cool, quiet and collected—always.

name: Malone, Fowler Reed
motto: "The envious man grows lean at the success of his neighbor."
clubs: Grader of papers in Junior Law
description: A long, lean fellow with a consuming love of law. One who never speaks unless he knows his subject.

name: Morgan, Fitz R.
motto: "Diffidence and awkwardness are the two antidotes to love."
clubs: Representative State Legislature from Carrol County
description: A law student who has already made himself felt in the State Legislature. A man of courteous demeanor, and what is more—ability.

name: Mounger, Henry Jr.
motto: "He clothed whatever subject he spoke upon in most eloquent words."
description: A man of perseverance, ability, and assurance. One who speaks well of everyone—even those who oppose him in politics.

name: Park, Floy B.
motto: "He followeth knowledge like a setting star."
description: A native of the Lone Star State with all his native state's aggressiveness. Quiet and industrious, he makes his presence felt wherever he may be.

name: Rogers, Jameson Gayle
motto: "Framed to make women false."
description: Originally from Washington and Lee, it took Gayle but a short time to become popular with his fellows, not to speak of the Co-eds. He loves equally well a lively dance, or a hard lesson in Contracts.

name: Simrall, James Jr.
motto: "Affliction may one day smile again, 'Til then sit thee down sorrow."
description: One of our most brilliant students. Not an idler but a lad who makes high grades with little effort. Always in a cheerful mood and with the student body in any undertaking.

name: Golden, John Mitchell
motto: "Love seldom haunts the breast where learning lies."
description: John first came among us several years ago and remained until Uncle Sam's first call. Determined to be a lawyer, he returned this year and completed his course with honors.

name: Hendrick, Leon F.
motto: "He seeks and he finds, he attacks and he conquers."
major: LL.B., A. B., Millsaps College, '16; Law School George Washington University '17
clubs: Hermaean Literary Society, Y. M. C. A.
description: A native of Jackson, but withal a good fellow, an industrious student, and a man of sterling worth. One who knows his own mind.

name: Smith, Wilburn Suber
motto: "I must be measured by my soul; The mind's the standard of the man."
description: His work is his one and only love. To never fall below A has been his aim—nor has he failed to reach it.

name: Wright, Donald Stevenson
motto: "An able man shows his spirit by gentle words and resolute actions; he is neither hot nor timid."
clubs: Secretary Treasurer Gordon Hall Boarding Club, '18
description: Almost every college honor has been thrust upon the back of this unprotesting student. Brilliant in intellect and upright in conduct he has made an excellent model for many a student.

name: Andrew, S. Scott
clubs: President of Junior Law Class Honor Council

name: Chester, L. Sumners
clubs: President of Phi Sigma First term '19

name: Charles, D. Williams
clubs: B.A. and M.A. Univ. of Virginia '17

name: Flynt, William B.
motto: "He danced, I say, right well, With emphasis, and also with good sense, A thing in footing indispensable."
description: "Bill" has great love for members of the fairer sex and equally strong hatred of mathematics. We can see him now as he shimmies over the Alps with his surveying instruments. An altogether lovable and well beloved student.

name: McDowell, William M.
motto: "For thy sake, tobacco, I would do anything but die."
description: "Mac" boasts of several months overseas and many and thrilling are the experiences he relates. His fondness for the ladies led him to coach the girls in basketball, at which he made a success. With his cheery nature and sunny disposition he will succeed in any of the walks of life.

name: Moser, Cecil R.
motto: "If honor lie in eating, he's right honorable."
description: Here is a student who truly believes that "Silence is Golden" and backs up this statement by astonishing grades in his work. No one would ever accuse him of being a Beau Brummel but embodied in him are all the elements which spell success in after life.

name: Linder, John Lee
motto: "Attempt the end and never stand to doubt, Nothing so hard but search will find it out."
description: He hails from the metropolis of Oxford and does not try to conceal the fact. His chief weakness is his fondness of playing ball with the Oxford "High Schoolers" but that he is respected by his classmates is attested by his being president of his class.

name: Applewhite, Joe Ross
motto: "He who is above his business will soon find his business above him."
description: A lad with a distinguished air. His derby, let it be known, lends him this air, and not his general appearance. All druggists have their specialties—his is rosewater.

name: Brooks, Selma Louise
motto: "Forward and frolic, glee was there, The will to do, the soul to dare."
description: A strong believer in "woman's rights," she believes that women pharmacists will soon be as plentiful as vampires. Despite her suffragist tendencies, she is one of the most popular girls on the campus.

name: Durfey, John Russell
motto: "The women pardoned all except his face."
description: The Art Editor of 'Ole Miss.' All he needs is a studio and a petite red bow tie. He already has a figure and face of the true artistic type. A man talented as few men are.

name: Hemphill, F. Perry
motto: "Conscious thought will overflow in words unconsciously."
description: "Pinkie," the boy with the home-spun countenance is one of the best pill-rollers of his class. A man of few words, but many actions.

name: Huff, John Charles
motto: "Sighed and looked, and sighed again."
description: Not a Hercules, nor yet an Apollo, but a lad who takes life seriously and who will get the most out of it. A capable, well liked student.

name: Lynch, Mike L.
clubs: Three years at A. & M. College
description: A man of self assurance and poise. One who believes in doing everything well, and who is willing to try anything once.

name: Mitchell, Townsend S.
motto: "As silent as the pictures on the wall."
description: One who prides himself not on his beauty, but on his brain. A steady, industrious lad whose grand passion is haunting the post-office window.

name: Moser, Clio S.
motto: "She's mine, and I am rich in having such a jewel."
description: An energetic young fellow who means business. One who has discovered that the secret of making A's is to study a little now and then.

name: Taylor, Gladys
motto: "Whoever loved, that loved not at first sight."
description: One of the most charming of the charming. A short while she dwelt among us—'til Cupid called, and now far-away Texas is her home. Wirt—you lucky, lazy man!

name: Featherstone, Lucius H.
motto: "Extremely precise is the order of his daily life."
description: He wears a dignity peculiar even unto himself. Unimpressed by the manifold charms of Eve—he wanders about cool, calm, collected—a man to be wondered at.

name: Sweatt, Dudley
motto: "Choose such pleasures as recreate much, and cost little."
description: He's a rara avis. He parts his hair in the middle and wonders why the ladies don't love him. Withal a modest, quiet fellow who will falter at nothing short of work. He seldom studies after the lights go off, though he might with ease (Rouge Cheveu).
